---
title: "Kelvin Wong"
image: profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/kelvin1189
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/your-handle
---

## About Me

I'm a senior specialist based in Taiwan working on life insurance risk management. This blog is where I work through ideas in actuarial modeling and quantitative risk management — usually with code, usually using public market data.

## What this blog covers

The recurring threads here are:

- **Yield curve modeling** — Dynamic Nelson-Siegel, CIR,Vasicek, and other interest rate models 
- **Insurance capital frameworks** — ICS, Solvency II, and scenario-based risk simulation
- **Quantitative risk modeling** — EWMA, GARCH, and other volatility models
- **Paper notes** — short summaries of papers I've worked through, with reproductions where useful

Everything published here is my own opinion and does not represent the views of any employer.

## Background

- *Master* M.Sc. in Risk Management and Insurance 
- *Candidate* of the *SOA*
- 3+ years working in life insurance solvency and capital modeling
- 6+ years working in risk management and capital modeling in P&C insurance
- 3 years of graduate research assistant of NSTC Research Project supported by the National Science Council in financial risk management and insurance
- Physics undergrad, with double major in Mathematics
- 10+ years of programming experience in R, Python, and SQL

## Get in touch

The fastest ways to reach me are GitHub (for code/blog corrections) or LinkedIn (for everything else). For technical errors in posts, opening a GitHub issue against the [blog repo](https://github.com/kelvin1189/actuarial-modeling-notes) is most efficient.
